content: What are the undergraduate admission requirements at LUMS?

The undergraduate admission requirements at LUMS include a Higher Secondary School Certificate (HSSC / A-Levels / IB) with at least 60%, SAT score (1200+ recommended) or NTS-NAT score, and completion of the LUMS Admission Test (LAT). Additionally, applicants must submit a personal statement and letters of recommendation, and may be required to attend an interview. The application deadlines are Early Decision (October 31) and Regular Decision (January 31).

What scholarships does LUMS offer for financially disadvantaged students?

LUMS offers several Need-Based Scholarships for financially disadvantaged students, including the LUMS National Outreach Programme (NOP), LUMS One Nation Programme, and General Need-Based Aid. Additionally, the university also offers Merit-Based Scholarships such as Chancellor's Award, Dean's Merit Scholarship, and Academic Excellence Award.
What is the attendance policy and what happens if I miss too many classes?

The attendance policy at LUMS requires a minimum of 80% attendance in all scheduled class sessions. Missing more than 20% of classes may result in grade deduction or a Withdrawal Failing (WF) status, and three consecutive unexcused absences will be reported to the Office of Student Affairs.
